confirmed.note: Entire chunked-batch streaming wrapper (struct *_stream + run_decode/open/feed/get_text/flush/close, 6 funcs) copied kyutai_stt -> moonshine_streaming with mechanical rename; only the inner *_transcribe call body diverges. Author even left comment 'Same shape as kyutai_stt_stream_*; see src/kyutai_stt.cpp'. partial-edited.

Per-backend dispatch block '#if __has_include("X.h")\n if(s->X_stream_state) return X_stream_OP(...)' duplicated for kyutai and moonshine across close/feed/get_text/flush/open. Parallel-branch within-file copy-paste of the routing boilerplate.

Three near-identical backend-init blocks: if(s->backend=="X"){ X_context_params p = X_default_params(); ...; s->X_ctx = X_init_from_file(model_path,p); if(!s->X_ctx){...} }. Copy-paste-with-rename across the 3 sibling backends in the same dispatch function.

false_positives.where: src/f5_tts.cpp (weight-loader block) vs src/moonshine_streaming.cpp / src/kyutai_stt.cpp loaders
false_positives.what_happened: ggml model files all populate a weights struct with long sequential runs of 'w.NAME = get("prefix.NAME.weight"); w.NAME_bias = get("prefix.NAME.bias");'. Lines differ only by the tensor-name string literal, so the token stream after identifier/string normalization collapses to a near-identical skeleton -> #056 token pass scores high cross-file similarity (f5_tts 30, moonshine 36, kyutai 9 such pairs). It is a name table, not transplanted logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Add a 'tensor-table / accessor-table' density filter to #056: if a candidate block's lines are dominated (>~70%) by a single repeated call shape whose only varying token is a string literal (assignment target = get(<string-literal>) idiom), down-weight or suppress. Concretely: when normalized tokens-per-line are near-constant AND >60% of distinct tokens are string literals, treat as data-table and require a higher w-threshold (e.g. >=0.97 verbatim) before reporting.

false_positives.where: src/kyutai_stt.cpp:36-78 sample_token vs other model files
false_positives.what_happened: sample_token (argmax-or-softmax-temperature sampler) is a self-contained ~40-line idiom that recurs nearly verbatim in many of the autoregressive model files (every TTS/ASR decoder needs it). Token detector flags it cross-file, but it is a standard utility every backend independently needs, not architectural drift introduced by this commit.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Maintain a small allowlist of well-known numeric idioms (softmax/argmax sampler, RoPE table fill, layernorm) OR better: archcheck should report such recurring small utilities once as a 'candidate for extraction to a shared header' (advisory) rather than as N copy-paste violations. Heuristic: if the same block recurs in >=3 files identically, reclassify from 'copy-paste pair' to single 'extract-to-common' suggestion, lowering noise.

intro_commits.reason: Both files entered the tree together via the whisper.cpp fork import (vendored ggml virtgpu/RPC backend); later refreshed by ggml vendor bumps 0f53dd55 and b3317bb2. The 2-cycle is upstream ggml virtgpu backend code, not authored CrispASR code.

cycle.evidence: Cycle is entirely inside ggml/src/ggml-virtgpu/ (vendored ggml backend). present=false because not authored code. archcheck --graph: sccs_cyclic=1, largest_scc=2.
